content.content:
1→package db
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→
     7→	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     8→	"github.com/riverqueue/river/riverdriver/riverpgxv5"
     9→	"github.com/riverqueue/river/rivermigrate"
    10→)
    11→
    12→const schema = `
    13→-- Tracked software projects (the central entity)
    14→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS projects (
    15→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    16→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    17→    description TEXT,
    18→    agent_prompt TEXT,
    19→    agent_rules JSONB,
    20→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    21→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    22→);
    23→
    24→-- Configured ingestion sources (polling-based: GitHub, Docker Hub)
    25→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sources (
    26→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    27→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    28→    provider VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    29→    repository VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    30→    poll_interval_seconds INT DEFAULT 86400,
    31→    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    32→    config JSONB,
    33→    last_polled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    34→    last_error TEXT,
    35→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    36→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    37→    UNIQUE(provider, repository)
    38→);
    39→
    40→-- Context sources (read-only references for agent research)
    41→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS context_sources (
    42→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    43→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    44→    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    45→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    46→    config JSONB NOT NULL,
    47→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    48→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    49→);
    50→
    51→-- Source-level releases (detected from polling sources)
    52→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS releases (
    53→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    54→    source_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    55→    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    56→    raw_data JSONB,
    57→    released_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    58→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    59→    UNIQUE(source_id, version)
    60→);
    61→
    62→-- Project-level semantic releases (AI-generated, correlating source releases)
    63→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS semantic_releases (
    64→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    66→    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    67→    report JSONB,
    68→    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    69→    error TEXT,
    70→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    71→    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    72→    UNIQUE(project_id, version)
    73→);
    74→
    75→-- Join table: which source releases compose a semantic release
    76→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS semantic_release_sources (
    77→    semantic_release_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    78→    release_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    79→    PRIMARY KEY (semantic_release_id, release_id)
    80→);
    81→
    82→-- Notification channels (standalone)
    83→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification_channels (
    84→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    85→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    86→    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    87→    config JSONB NOT NULL,
    88→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    89→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    90→);
    91→
    92→-- Subscriptions: two types (source releases and semantic releases)
    93→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS subscriptions (
    94→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    95→    channel_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES notification_channels(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    96→    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('source_release', 'semantic_release')),
    97→    source_id UUID REFERENCES sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    98→    project_id UUID REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    99→    version_filter TEXT,
   100→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   101→    CHECK (
   102→        (type = 'source_release'   AND source_id  IS NOT NULL AND project_id IS NULL) OR
   103→        (type = 'semantic_release' AND project_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id  IS NULL)
   104→    )
   105→);
   106→
   107→-- Agent runs (scoped to project)
   108→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_runs (
   109→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   110→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   111→    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   112→    trigger VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   113→    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   114→    prompt_used TEXT,
   115→    error TEXT,
   116→    started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   117→    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   118→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   119→);
   120→
   121→ALTER TABLE agent_runs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version VARCHAR(100);
   122→
   123→-- API authentication keys
   124→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS api_keys (
   125→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   126→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   127→    key_hash VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   128→    key_prefix VARCHAR(12) NOT NULL,
   129→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   130→    last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ
   131→);
   132→
   133→-- Trigger for SSE: notify on new releases
   134→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION notify_release_created() RETURNS trigger AS $$
   135→BEGIN
   136→    PERFORM pg_notify('release_events', json_build_object('type', 'release', 'id', NEW.id)::text);
   137→    RETURN NEW;
   138→END;
   139→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   140→
   141→DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS release_created_trigger ON releases;
   142→CREATE TRIGGER release_created_trigger
   143→    AFTER INSERT ON releases
   144→    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION notify_release_created();
   145→
   146→-- Trigger for SSE: notify on semantic release completion
   147→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION notify_semantic_release() RETURNS trigger AS $$
   148→BEGIN
   149→    IF NEW.status = 'completed' AND (OLD IS NULL OR OLD.status != 'completed') THEN
   150→        PERFORM pg_notify('release_events', json_build_object('type', 'semantic_release', 'id', NEW.id)::text);
   151→    END IF;
   152→    RETURN NEW;
   153→END;
   154→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   155→
   156→DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS semantic_release_trigger ON semantic_releases;
   157→CREATE TRIGGER semantic_release_trigger
   158→    AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON semantic_releases
   159→    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION notify_semantic_release();
   160→
   161→-- Release TODOs (acknowledge/resolve tracking)
   162→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS release_todos (
   163→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   164→    release_id UUID REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   165→    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   166→    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   167→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   168→    acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   169→    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   170→    CHECK (
   171→        (release_id IS NOT NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NULL) OR
   172→        (release_id IS NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL)
   173→    )
   174→);
   175→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_release_todos_release_id ON release_todos(release_id) WHERE release_id IS NOT NULL;
   176→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_release_todos_semantic_release_id ON release_todos(semantic_release_id) WHERE semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL;
   177→
   178→-- Onboarding scans (GitHub repo dependency detection)
   179→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS onboard_scans (
   180→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   181→    repo_url TEXT NOT NULL,
   182→    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   183→    results JSONB,
   184→    error TEXT,
   185→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   186→    started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   187→    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
   188→);
   189→
   190→-- Authenticated users (GitHub OAuth)
   191→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
   192→    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   193→    github_id       BIGINT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   194→    github_login    VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   195→    name            VARCHAR(200),
   196→    avatar_url      TEXT,
   197→    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   198→    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   199→);
   200→
   201→-- User sessions (server-side, referenced by HttpOnly cookie)
   202→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
   203→    id          UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   204→    user_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   205→    expires_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
   206→    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   207→);
   208→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_user_id ON sessions(user_id);
   209→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_expires_at ON sessions(expires_at);
   210→
   211→-- Release gates (per-project gate configuration)
   212→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS release_gates (
   213→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   214→    project_id UUID NOT NULL UNIQUE REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   215→    required_sources JSONB,
   216→    timeout_hours INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 168,
   217→    version_mapping JSONB,
   218→    nl_rule TEXT,
   219→    enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
   220→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   221→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   222→);
   223→
   224→-- Version readiness (per-version gate state tracking)
   225→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS version_readiness (
   226→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   227→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   228→    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   229→    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
   230→        CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'ready', 'timed_out')),
   231→    sources_met JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
   232→    sources_missing JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
   233→    nl_rule_passed BOOLEAN,
   234→    timeout_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
   235→    opened_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   236→    agent_triggered BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
   237→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   238→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   239→    UNIQUE(project_id, version)
   240→);
   241→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_version_readiness_timeout
   242→    ON version_readiness(timeout_at) WHERE status = 'pending';
   243→
   244→-- Gate events (audit log for gate lifecycle)
   245→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS gate_events (
   246→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   247→    version_readiness_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES version_readiness(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   248→    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   249→    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   250→    event_type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
   251→    source_id UUID,
   252→    details JSONB,
   253→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   254→);
   255→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gate_events_readiness ON gate_events(version_readiness_id, created_at);
   256→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gate_events_project ON gate_events(project_id, created_at);
   257→`
   258→
   259→// RunMigrations applies River's schema and the application schema. Idempotent — safe to call on every startup.
   260→func RunMigrations(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool) error {
   261→	migrator, err := rivermigrate.New(riverpgxv5.New(pool), nil)
   262→	if err != nil {
   263→		return fmt.Errorf("create river migrator: %w", err)
   264→	}
   265→	if _, err := migrator.Migrate(ctx, rivermigrate.DirectionUp, nil); err != nil {
   266→		return fmt.Errorf("river migrations: %w", err)
   267→	}
   268→
   269→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, schema); err != nil {
   270→		return fmt.Errorf("app migrations: %w", err)
   271→	}
   272→
   273→	// Migrate existing subscription type values: source→source_release, project→semantic_release.
   274→	// Drop old CHECK constraints first (both the named type check and the unnamed
   275→	// composite check referencing old values), then UPDATE data, then re-add constraints.
   276→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   277→		ALTER TABLE subscriptions DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS subscriptions_type_check;
   278→		ALTER TABLE subscriptions DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS subscriptions_check;
   279→		UPDATE subscriptions SET type = 'source_release' WHERE type = 'source';
   280→		UPDATE subscriptions SET type = 'semantic_release' WHERE type = 'project';
   281→		ALTER TABLE subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT subscriptions_type_check CHECK (type IN ('source_release', 'semantic_release'));
   282→		ALTER TABLE subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT subscriptions_check CHECK (
   283→			(type = 'source_release'   AND source_id IS NOT NULL AND project_id IS NULL) OR
   284→			(type = 'semantic_release' AND project_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id IS NULL)
   285→		);
   286→	`); err != nil {
   287→		return fmt.Errorf("subscription type migration: %w", err)
   288→	}
   289→
   290→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   291→		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version_filter_include TEXT;
   292→		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version_filter_exclude TEXT;
   293→	`); err != nil {
   294→		return fmt.Errorf("source version filter migration: %w", err)
   295→	}
   296→
   297→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   298→		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS exclude_prereleases BOOLEAN DEFAULT false;
   299→	`); err != nil {
   300→		return fmt.Errorf("source exclude_prereleases migration: %w", err)
   301→	}
   302→
   303→	// Auto-create release_gates for projects with WaitForAllSources enabled.
   304→	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   305→		INSERT INTO release_gates (project_id, timeout_hours, enabled)
   306→		SELECT p.id, 168, true
   307→		FROM projects p
   308→		WHERE p.agent_rules->>'wait_for_all_sources' = 'true'
   309→		ON CONFLICT (project_id) DO NOTHING
   310→	`); err != nil {
   311→		return fmt.Errorf("wait_for_all_sources migration: %w", err)
   312→	}
   313→
   314→	return nil
   315→}
   316→

input.command:
cat > /tmp/trend_analysis.txt << 'EOF'
=== RELEASE TREND CHART DATA FLOW ANALYSIS ===

FRONTEND FLOW:
==============

1. COMPONENT: /web/components/dashboard/release-trend-chart.tsx
   - Component renders a bar chart showing release trends
   - Uses Recharts library for visualization
   - Supports 4 time ranges with different granularities:
     * 7d (daily)
     * 30d (daily)
     * 90d (weekly)
     * 1y / 365d (monthly)

2. API CALL: system.trend(granularity, days)
   - Location: /web/lib/api/client.ts:239-240
   - Endpoint: GET /api/v1/stats/trend?granularity=${granularity}&days=${days}
   - Caching: SWR with 30-second refresh interval
   - Cache key: `trend-${granularity}-${days}`

3. DATA TRANSFORMATION:
   - Frontend receives TrendData with buckets array
   - Each bucket has: period (YYYY-MM-DD), releases, semantic_releases
   - formatPeriod() converts YYYY-MM-DD to display format
   - Chart data maps period to label for display

API LAYER:
==========

4. HANDLER: /internal/api/health.go - Trend()
   - Route: GET /api/v1/stats/trend (line 162 in server.go)
   - Input validation:
     * granularity defaults to "daily"
     * validates: daily, weekly, monthly (400 if invalid)
     * days defaults to 7
     * validates: 1-365 (400 if invalid)
   - Date calculation:
     * start = NOW().UTC() - days
     * end = NOW().UTC()
   - Calls database layer: h.checker.GetTrend(ctx, granularity, start, now)
   - Response: TrendResponse{Granularity, Buckets[]}

DATABASE LAYER:
===============

5. QUERY: /internal/api/pgstore.go - GetTrend()
   - Implements HealthChecker interface
   - Granularity validation: day, week, month (error if invalid)
   - SQL query uses PostgreSQL generate_series to create complete date buckets
   - Query structure:
     a) CTE "buckets": generates series from start to end at granularity interval
     b) LEFT JOIN releases: counts releases where released_at or created_at in range
     c) LEFT JOIN semantic_releases: counts completed semantic releases
     d) Returns 0 for missing buckets (COALESCE)
   - Period format: YYYY-MM-DD (cast to date)
   - Result: []TrendBucket with Period, Releases, SemanticReleases counts

DATABASE SCHEMA:
================

releases table:
  - source_id UUID (FK to sources)
  - version VARCHAR
  - released_at TIMESTAMPTZ (nullable)
  - created_at TIMESTAMPTZ (default NOW())

semantic_releases table:
  - project_id UUID (FK to projects)
  - version VARCHAR
  - status VARCHAR (pending, completed, etc.)
  - created_at TIMESTAMPTZ (default NOW())
  - completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ (nullable)

POTENTIAL CORRECTNESS ISSUES:
=============================

1. TIMEZONE HANDLING MISMATCH:
   - Backend: Uses NOW().UTC() for time calculation
   - Frontend: Assumes period strings are in system timezone
   - formatPeriod() does: new Date(period + "T00:00:00")
   - This could create timezone offset issues if frontend is not in UTC
   - Example: Period "2026-03-26" might render as "2026-03-25" on non-UTC systems

2. EDGE CASE: End-of-period bucket truncation
   - Query uses COALESCE(released_at, created_at) >= $1 AND < $2
   - If a release has released_at = NULL (orphaned), uses created_at
   - For weekly/monthly: date_trunc truncates to start of period
   - Example: Release on 2026-03-26 22:00:00 in weekly mode
     * date_trunc('week', timestamp) returns 2026-03-23 00:00:00 (Monday)
     * So it correctly groups in same week, but format may be confusing

3. FILTER DISCREPANCY: Released vs Created date
   - releases.released_at may be NULL, defaults to created_at
   - But semantic_releases ONLY uses created_at
   - This asymmetry could cause confusion:
     * Release created on Mar 26 but released May 1: counts in May trend
     * But semantic release for same version: counts in March trend
   - Creates potential mismatch in dashboards

4. MISSING VALIDATION: Empty result handling
   - If no releases in range: query returns empty buckets array
   - Frontend shows "No Data" state (line 116 in release-trend-chart.tsx)
   - But buckets with 0 values are included (COALESCE fills zeros)
   - So empty result means no releases at all, not missing data

5. GRANULARITY OUTPUT FORMAT INCONSISTENCY:
   - Daily granularity: "Jan 29" format fits well
   - Weekly granularity: shows start-of-week date, but label is "Jan 29" not "week of Jan 29"
   - Monthly granularity: "Jan '26" format, but based on date_trunc('month')
   - User may not realize "Jan 29" in weekly view is actually week-of Jan 29

6. MISSING FILTERING: Query doesn't filter by active/enabled status
   - Counts releases from ALL sources, regardless of enabled status
   - Counts semantic releases from ALL projects, regardless of active
   - May show old data from disabled sources as current trends

7. PERFORMANCE: N+1 hidden in period generation
   - generate_series generates every single day/week/month in range
   - 365-day request generates 365 CTE rows, then LEFT JOINs to aggregate
   - For 1-year requests this is significant but acceptable
   - Could be optimized if release counts were pre-aggregated

QUERY CORRECTNESS ANALYSIS:
===========================

The SQL query logic appears sound:

```sql
WITH buckets AS (
  SELECT date_trunc('%s', gs)::date AS period
  FROM generate_series($1::timestamptz, $2::timestamptz, '1 %s'::interval) gs
)
SELECT
  b.period,
  COALESCE(r.cnt, 0) AS releases,
  COALESCE(sr.cnt, 0) AS semantic_releases
FROM buckets b
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT date_trunc('%s', COALESCE(released_at, created_at))::date AS period,
         COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM releases
  WHERE COALESCE(released_at, created_at) >= $1 AND COALESCE(released_at, created_at) < $2
  GROUP BY 1
) r ON r.period = b.period
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT date_trunc('%s', created_at)::date AS period,
         COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM semantic_releases
  WHERE created_at >= $1 AND created_at < $2
  GROUP BY 1
) sr ON sr.period = b.period
ORDER BY b.period
```

✓ Properly handles missing periods with LEFT JOIN + COALESCE
✓ Filter conditions are correct (>= start, < end)
✓ Date truncation matches the granularity parameter
✓ Aggregation groups correctly by truncated date
✓ Results ordered chronologically

BUT:
✗ Asymmetric date handling: releases use COALESCE, semantic_releases use created_at only
✗ No visibility into what "status" for semantic_releases (implicitly counts all)
✗ Released_at = NULL behavior may cause confusion

TESTING GAP:
============
- No integration tests for GetTrend in pgstore
- health_test.go only tests HTTP handler, not database query
- No tests verify:
  * Timezone handling
  * Bucket completeness (all periods present)
  * Released_at vs created_at edge cases
  * Filtering behavior across enabled/disabled entities

FRONTEND ISSUES:
================
1. formatPeriod() assumes UTC but doesn't validate
2. Weekly display shows date but not "week of" label
3. Cache key includes granularity+days but no timezone info
4. No loading error state (only loading spinner)
5. SWR 30-second refresh may show stale trend for 30 seconds after release event
EOF
cat /tmp/trend_analysis.txt

data.prompt:
Explore how the "Release Trend" chart works on the dashboard. Look for:

1. The frontend chart component that renders the trend (search in web/components/dashboard/)
2. The API endpoint and backend handler for trend data (search for "trend", "stats/trend")
3. The database query that generates trend data
4. How the data is aggregated (daily/weekly/monthly counts)

Be thorough - trace from frontend → API call → backend handler → SQL query. Report the full data flow and any potential issues with correctness.

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me create the final comprehensive report:

## Release Trend Chart - Complete Data Flow Analysis

I've traced the Release Trend chart from frontend through to the database. Here's the complete picture:

### Frontend → API → Database Flow

**Frontend Component** (`/web/components/dashboard/release-trend-chart.tsx`):
- Renders a Recharts bar chart with two data series: `releases` and `semantic_releases`
- Supports 4 selectable time ranges (7d, 30d, 90d, 1y) with corresponding granularities (daily, weekly, monthly)
- Makes API call via SWR: `system.trend(granularity, days)` with 30-second refresh interval
- Cache key: `trend-${granularity}-${days}` for independent caching per granularity/range combo
- Transforms backend response: period (YYYY-MM-DD) → formatted label via `formatPeriod()`

**API Handler** (`/internal/api/health.go:Trend()`):
- Route: `GET /api/v1/stats/trend` (registered line 162 in server.go)
- Validates granularity: "daily", "weekly", or "monthly" (defaults to daily, returns 400 if invalid)
- Validates days: 1-365 (defaults to 7, returns 400 if out of range)
- Calculates time window: `start = now.UTC() - days`, `end = now.UTC()`
- Delegates to: `h.checker.GetTrend(ctx, granularity, start, now)`
- Returns: `TrendResponse{Granularity, Buckets: []TrendBucket}`

**Database Query** (`/internal/api/pgstore.go:GetTrend()`):
```go
// Converts granularity param to PostgreSQL date_trunc argument
case "daily": trunc = "day"
case "weekly": trunc = "week"  
case "monthly": trunc = "month"

// Generates complete time series with generate_series CTE
// LEFT JOINs releases & semantic_releases to fill gaps with 0 counts
```

The SQL query structure:
1. **CTE "buckets"**: Generates every period (day/week/month) from start to end using `generate_series(..., '1 day|week|month'::interval)`
2. **LEFT JOIN releases**: Groups by date-truncated `COALESCE(released_at, created_at)`, counts per period
3. **LEFT JOIN semantic_releases**: Groups by date-truncated `created_at`, counts per period
4. **Result**: Complete time series with zeros for empty periods via `COALESCE(r.cnt, 0)` and `COALESCE(sr.cnt, 0)`

### Potential Correctness Issues

**1. Timezone Handling Mismatch** ⚠️ **Critical**
- Backend uses `NOW().UTC()` for calculations in UTC
- Frontend: `formatPeriod()` does `new Date(period + "T00:00:00")` assuming UTC
- If browser is in non-UTC timezone, this creates offset bugs: "2026-03-26" could render as "2026-03-25" 
- Period strings are ISO date format (YYYY-MM-DD) with no timezone hint

**2. Asymmetric Date Semantics** ⚠️ **High**
- **Releases**: Uses `COALESCE(released_at, created_at)` — can count based on either field
- **Semantic Releases**: Uses ONLY `created_at`
- This creates a subtle mismatch:
  - A release with `released_at = 2026-05-01` but `created_at = 2026-03-26` will appear in May trend
  - A semantic release for the same version will appear in March trend
  - Users see inconsistent aggregation across the two data series

**3. No Status Filtering** ⚠️ **Medium**
- Semantic releases are counted regardless of status (pending, completed, failed, etc.)
- Suggests ALL semantic releases are included, not just completed ones
- Could skew trend with incomplete/failed agent runs

**4. Disabled Sources Not Excluded** ⚠️ **Medium**
- Query doesn't filter by `sources.enabled = true`
- Counts releases from disabled sources
- May show trend data from archived/inactive projects

**5. Granularity Label Mismatch** ⚠️ **Low**
- Weekly display shows date "Jan 29" but doesn't clarify it's week-starting date
- User may think "Jan 29" means that specific day, not the week of
- Monthly display shows "Jan '26" but `date_trunc('month')` returns 2026-01-01

**6. Empty Bucket Generation Overhead** 📊
- `generate_series` creates 365 rows for 1-year requests
- Then LEFT JOINs aggregate to them
- Not a bug, but unoptimized (could pre-aggregate daily counts into a materialized view)

**7. No Error State on Frontend** 📱
- Component shows loading spinner or "No Data" but no error handling
- If API returns 500, user sees spinning loader indefinitely

### Database Schema

**Releases table**: 
- `released_at TIMESTAMPTZ` (nullable) — when release was published upstream
- `created_at TIMESTAMPTZ` (default NOW()) — when we detected it

**Semantic_releases table**:
- `created_at TIMESTAMPTZ` (default NOW()) — when semantic release was created
- `completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ` (nullable) — when agent analysis completed
- `status VARCHAR` — but not filtered in query

### Testing Gaps

- No integration tests for `GetTrend()` database function
- `health_test.go` only tests HTTP handler logic, not DB query correctness
- Missing test coverage for:
  - Timezone behavior
  - Bucket completeness (all periods returned)
  - `released_at = NULL` edge cases
  - Enabled/disabled filtering
  - Status-based filtering

### Summary of Files

**Frontend:**
- `/web/components/dashboard/release-trend-chart.tsx` — Chart component
- `/web/lib/api/client.ts:239-240` — API client (`system.trend()`)
- `/web/lib/api/types.ts:233-242` — TrendBucket & TrendData types

**Backend:**
- `/internal/api/server.go:162` — Route registration
- `/internal/api/health.go:80-120` — HTTP handler (Trend)
- `/internal/api/pgstore.go:1214-1275` — Database query (GetTrend)

**Database:**
- `/internal/db/migrations.go:52-73` — Schema (releases & semantic_releases tables)

The query logic itself is sound, but the **timezone handling and asymmetric date semantics create data consistency risks**, especially if the frontend and backend operate in different timezones or if `released_at` is regularly NULL for releases.

4. **Result**: Complete time series with zeros for empty periods via `COALESCE(r.cnt, 0)` and `COALESCE(sr.cnt, 0)`

### Potential Correctness Issues

**1. Timezone Handling Mismatch** ⚠️ **Critical**
- Backend uses `NOW().UTC()` for calculations in UTC
- Frontend: `formatPeriod()` does `new Date(period + "T00:00:00")` assuming UTC
- If browser is in non-UTC timezone, this creates offset bugs: "2026-03-26" could render as "2026-03-25" 
- Period strings are ISO date format (YYYY-MM-DD) with no timezone hint

**2. Asymmetric Date Semantics** ⚠️ **High**
- **Releases**: Uses `COALESCE(released_at, created_at)` — can count based on either field
- **Semantic Releases**: Uses ONLY `created_at`
- This creates a subtle mismatch:
  - A release with `released_at = 2026-05-01` but `created_at = 2026-03-26` will appear in May trend
  - A semantic release for the same version will appear in March trend
  - Users see inconsistent aggregation across the two data series

**3. No Status Filtering** ⚠️ **Medium**
- Semantic releases are counted regardless of status (pending, completed, failed, etc.)
- Suggests ALL semantic releases are included, not just completed ones
- Could skew trend with incomplete/failed agent runs

**4. Disabled Sources Not Excluded** ⚠️ **Medium**
- Query doesn't filter by `sources.enabled = true`
- Counts releases from disabled sources
- May show trend data from archived/inactive projects

**5. Granularity Label Mismatch** ⚠️ **Low**
- Weekly display shows date "Jan 29" but doesn't clarify it's week-starting date
- User may think "Jan 29" means that specific day, not the week of
- Monthly display shows "Jan '26" but `date_trunc('month')` returns 2026-01-01

**6. Empty Bucket Generation Overhead** 📊
- `generate_series` creates 365 rows for 1-year requests
- Then LEFT JOINs aggregate to them
- Not a bug, but unoptimized (could pre-aggregate daily counts into a materialized view)

**7. No Error State on Frontend** 📱
- Component shows loading spinner or "No Data" but no error handling
- If API returns 500, user sees spinning loader indefinitely

### Database Schema

**Releases table**: 
- `released_at TIMESTAMPTZ` (nullable) — when release was published upstream
- `created_at TIMESTAMPTZ` (default NOW()) — when we detected it

**Semantic_releases table**:
- `created_at TIMESTAMPTZ` (default NOW()) — when semantic release was created
- `completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ` (nullable) — when agent analysis completed
- `status VARCHAR` — but not filtered in query

### Testing Gaps

- No integration tests for `GetTrend()` database function
- `health_test.go` only tests HTTP handler logic, not DB query correctness
- Missing test coverage for:
  - Timezone behavior
  - Bucket completeness (all periods returned)
  - `released_at = NULL` edge cases
  - Enabled/disabled filtering
  - Status-based filtering

### Summary of Files

**Frontend:**
- `/web/components/dashboard/release-trend-chart.tsx` — Chart component
- `/web/lib/api/client.ts:239-240` — API client (`system.trend()`)
- `/web/lib/api/types.ts:233-242` — TrendBucket & TrendData types

**Backend:**
- `/internal/api/server.go:162` — Route registration
- `/internal/api/health.go:80-120` — HTTP handler (Trend)
- `/internal/api/pgstore.go:1214-1275` — Database query (GetTrend)

**Database:**
- `/internal/db/migrations.go:52-73` — Schema (releases & semantic_releases tables)

The query logic itself is sound, but the **timezone handling and asymmetric date semantics create data consistency risks**, especially if the frontend and backend operate in different timezones or if `released_at` is regularly NULL for releases.

I investigated the release trend chart. The core query logic is sound — it uses `generate_series` to create complete time buckets and LEFT JOINs release/semantic release counts. Here are the findings:

**The trend data is mostly correct**, but there are a few issues worth noting:

1. **No version filter exclusions** — The trend query counts ALL releases including ones filtered out by source version rules (`version_filter_include`/`version_filter_exclude`). The Recent Activity feed and project release lists properly exclude these, but the trend chart doesn't. This could inflate the counts.

2. **No status filtering on semantic releases** — Failed/pending semantic releases are counted alongside completed ones, which could overstate the semantic release trend.

3. **Disabled sources not excluded** — Releases from disabled sources are still counted in the trend.

These are relatively minor — the chart still reflects actual database activity. Want me to fix any of these, or is the trend you're seeing off in a more specific way? If you can describe what looks wrong, I can dig into the exact issue.

message.content: That's Ok, I feel the "show excluded" in the releases in too add-hoc, can we introduce a global advanced filter component that can support filter by project, excluded, release date etc. then use it both in the releases page and the todo page

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/5.0.6/skills/brainstorming

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Offer visual companion** (if topic will involve visual questions) — this is its own message, not combined with a clarifying question. See the Visual Companion section below.
3. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
4. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
5. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
6. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
7. **Spec self-review** — quick inline check for placeholders, contradictions, ambiguity, scope (see below)
8. **User reviews written spec** — ask user to review the spec file before proceeding
9. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Visual questions ahead?" [shape=diamond];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)" [shape=box];
    "User reviews spec?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Visual questions ahead?";
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [label="yes"];
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Ask clarifying questions" [label="no"];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)";
    "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)" -> "User reviews spec?";
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Write design doc" [label="changes requested"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill" [label="approved"];
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**

- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Before asking detailed questions, assess scope: if the request describes multiple independent subsystems (e.g., "build a platform with chat, file storage, billing, and analytics"), flag this immediately. Don't spend questions refining details of a project that needs to be decomposed first.
- If the project is too large for a single spec, help the user decompose into sub-projects: what are the independent pieces, how do they relate, what order should they be built? Then brainstorm the first sub-project through the normal design flow. Each sub-project gets its own spec → plan → implementation cycle.
- For appropriately-scoped projects, ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**

- Propose 2-3 different approaches with trade-offs
- Present options conversationally with your recommendation and reasoning
- Lead with your recommended option and explain why

**Presenting the design:**

- Once you believe you understand what you're building, present the design
- Scale each section to its complexity: a few sentences if straightforward, up to 200-300 words if nuanced
- Ask after each section whether it looks right so far
- Cover: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing
- Be ready to go back and clarify if something doesn't make sense

**Design for isolation and clarity:**

- Break the system into smaller units that each have one clear purpose, communicate through well-defined interfaces, and can be understood and tested independently
- For each unit, you should be able to answer: what does it do, how do you use it, and what does it depend on?
- Can someone understand what a unit does without reading its internals? Can you change the internals without breaking consumers? If not, the boundaries need work.
- Smaller, well-bounded units are also easier for you to work with - you reason better about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. When a file grows large, that's often a signal that it's doing too much.

**Working in existing codebases:**

- Explore the current structure before proposing changes. Follow existing patterns.
- Where existing code has problems that affect the work (e.g., a file that's grown too large, unclear boundaries, tangled responsibilities), include targeted improvements as part of the design - the way a good developer improves code they're working in.
- Don't propose unrelated refactoring. Stay focused on what serves the current goal.

## After the Design

**Documentation:**

- Write the validated design (spec) to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
  - (User preferences for spec location override this default)
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Spec Self-Review:**
After writing the spec document, look at it with fresh eyes:

1. **Placeholder scan:** Any "TBD", "TODO", incomplete sections, or vague requirements? Fix them.
2. **Internal consistency:** Do any sections contradict each other? Does the architecture match the feature descriptions?
3. **Scope check:** Is this focused enough for a single implementation plan, or does it need decomposition?
4. **Ambiguity check:** Could any requirement be interpreted two different ways? If so, pick one and make it explicit.

Fix any issues inline. No need to re-review — just fix and move on.

**User Review Gate:**
After the spec review loop passes, ask the user to review the written spec before proceeding:

> "Spec written and committed to `<path>`. Please review it and let me know if you want to make any changes before we start writing out the implementation plan."

Wait for the user's response. If they request changes, make them and re-run the spec review loop. Only proceed once the user approves.

**Implementation:**

- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

## Visual Companion

A browser-based companion for showing mockups, diagrams, and visual options during brainstorming. Available as a tool — not a mode. Accepting the companion means it's available for questions that benefit from visual treatment; it does NOT mean every question goes through the browser.

**Offering the companion:** When you anticipate that upcoming questions will involve visual content (mockups, layouts, diagrams), offer it once for consent:
> "Some of what we're working on might be easier to explain if I can show it to you in a web browser. I can put together mockups, diagrams, comparisons, and other visuals as we go. This feature is still new and can be token-intensive. Want to try it? (Requires opening a local URL)"

**This offer MUST be its own message.** Do not combine it with clarifying questions, context summaries, or any other content. The message should contain ONLY the offer above and nothing else. Wait for the user's response before continuing. If they decline, proceed with text-only brainstorming.

**Per-question decision:** Even after the user accepts, decide FOR EACH QUESTION whether to use the browser or the terminal. The test: **would the user understand this better by seeing it than reading it?**

- **Use the browser** for content that IS visual — mockups, wireframes, layout comparisons, architecture diagrams, side-by-side visual designs
- **Use the terminal** for content that is text — requirements questions, conceptual choices, tradeoff lists, A/B/C/D text options, scope decisions

A question about a UI topic is not automatically a visual question. "What does personality mean in this context?" is a conceptual question — use the terminal. "Which wizard layout works better?" is a visual question — use the browser.

If they agree to the companion, read the detailed guide before proceeding:
`skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md`

content.content:
1→// web/lib/api/client.ts
     2→import type {
     3→  ApiResponse,
     4→  Project,
     5→  ProjectInput,
     6→  Release,
     7→  Source,
     8→  SourceInput,
     9→  Subscription,
    10→  SubscriptionInput,
    11→  BatchSubscriptionInput,
    12→  BatchDeleteSubscriptionInput,
    13→  NotificationChannel,
    14→  ChannelInput,
    15→  ContextSource,
    16→  ContextSourceInput,
    17→  SemanticRelease,
    18→  AgentRun,
    19→  HealthStatus,
    20→  Stats,
    21→  TrendData,
    22→  DiscoverItem,
    23→  Todo,
    24→  OnboardScan,
    25→  OnboardSelection,
    26→  OnboardApplyResult,
    27→  SuggestionItem,
    28→  RepoItem,
    29→  ReleaseGate,
    30→  ReleaseGateInput,
    31→  VersionReadiness,
    32→  GateEvent,
    33→} from "./types";
    34→
    35→const BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "/api/v1";
    36→
    37→async function request<T>(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {
    38→  const res = await fetch(`${BASE}${path}`, {
    39→    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", ...init?.headers },
    40→    ...init,
    41→  });
    42→  if (!res.ok) {
    43→    const body = await res.json().catch(() => null);
    44→    throw new Error(body?.error?.message ?? `Request failed: ${res.status}`);
    45→  }
    46→  if (res.status === 204) return null as T;
    47→  return res.json();
    48→}
    49→
    50→// --- Projects ---
    51→
    52→export const projects = {
    53→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
    54→    request<ApiResponse<Project[]>>(`/projects?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
    55→  get: (id: string) =>
    56→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>(`/projects/${id}`),
    57→  create: (input: ProjectInput) =>
    58→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>("/projects", {
    59→      method: "POST",
    60→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    61→    }),
    62→  update: (id: string, input: ProjectInput) =>
    63→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>(`/projects/${id}`, {
    64→      method: "PUT",
    65→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    66→    }),
    67→  delete: (id: string) =>
    68→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/projects/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
    69→};
    70→
    71→// --- Sources (nested under projects) ---
    72→
    73→export const sources = {
    74→  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>
    75→    request<ApiResponse<Source[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources?page=${page}`),
    76→  create: (projectId: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    77→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources`, {
    78→      method: "POST",
    79→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    80→    }),
    81→  get: (id: string) =>
    82→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/sources/${id}`),
    83→  update: (id: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    84→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/sources/${id}`, {
    85→      method: "PUT",
    86→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    87→    }),
    88→  delete: (id: string) =>
    89→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/sources/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
    90→  poll: (id: string) =>
    91→    request<ApiResponse<{ new_releases: number }>>(`/sources/${id}/poll`, {
    92→      method: "POST",
    93→    }),
    94→};
    95→
    96→export const releases = {
    97→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25, includeExcluded = false) =>
    98→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(
    99→      `/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}${includeExcluded ? '&include_excluded=true' : ''}`
   100→    ),
   101→  listBySource: (sourceId: string, page = 1) =>
   102→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/sources/${sourceId}/releases?page=${page}`),
   103→  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25, includeExcluded = false) =>
   104→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(
   105→      `/projects/${projectId}/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}${includeExcluded ? '&include_excluded=true' : ''}`
   106→    ),
   107→  get: (id: string) =>
   108→    request<ApiResponse<Release>>(`/releases/${id}`),
   109→};
   110→
   111→// --- Context Sources ---
   112→
   113→export const contextSources = {
   114→  list: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>
   115→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/context-sources?page=${page}`),
   116→  create: (projectId: string, input: ContextSourceInput) =>
   117→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource>>(`/projects/${projectId}/context-sources`, {
   118→      method: "POST",
   119→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   120→    }),
   121→  get: (id: string) =>
   122→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource>>(`/context-sources/${id}`),
   123→  update: (id: string, input: ContextSourceInput) =>
   124→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource>>(`/context-sources/${id}`, {
   125→      method: "PUT",
   126→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   127→    }),
   128→  delete: (id: string) =>
   129→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/context-sources/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   130→};
   131→
   132→// --- Semantic Releases ---
   133→
   134→export const semanticReleases = {
   135→  listAll: (page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
   136→    request<ApiResponse<SemanticRelease[]>>(`/semantic-releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   137→  list: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
   138→    request<ApiResponse<SemanticRelease[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/semantic-releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   139→  get: (id: string) =>
   140→    request<ApiResponse<SemanticRelease>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}`),
   141→  delete: (id: string) =>
   142→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   143→  getSources: (id: string) =>
   144→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}/sources`),
   145→};
   146→
   147→// --- Agent ---
   148→
   149→export const agent = {
   150→  triggerRun: (projectId: string, version?: string) =>
   151→    request<ApiResponse<AgentRun>>(`/projects/${projectId}/agent/run`, {
   152→      method: "POST",
   153→      body: JSON.stringify({ trigger: "test", version: version || undefined }),
   154→    }),
   155→  listRuns: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>
   156→    request<ApiResponse<AgentRun[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/agent/runs?page=${page}`),
   157→  getRun: (id: string) =>
   158→    request<ApiResponse<AgentRun>>(`/agent-runs/${id}`),
   159→};
   160→
   161→// --- Subscriptions ---
   162→
   163→export const subscriptions = {
   164→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 100) =>
   165→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription[]>>(`/subscriptions?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   166→  get: (id: string) => request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>(`/subscriptions/${id}`),
   167→  create: (input: SubscriptionInput) =>
   168→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>("/subscriptions", {
   169→      method: "POST",
   170→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   171→    }),
   172→  batchCreate: (input: BatchSubscriptionInput) =>
   173→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription[]>>("/subscriptions/batch", {
   174→      method: "POST",
   175→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   176→    }),
   177→  update: (id: string, input: SubscriptionInput) =>
   178→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>(`/subscriptions/${id}`, {
   179→      method: "PUT",
   180→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   181→    }),
   182→  delete: (id: string) =>
   183→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/subscriptions/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   184→  batchDelete: (input: BatchDeleteSubscriptionInput) =>
   185→    request<ApiResponse<null>>("/subscriptions/batch", {
   186→      method: "DELETE",
   187→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   188→    }),
   189→};
   190→
   191→// --- Notification Channels ---
   192→
   193→export const channels = {
   194→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 100) =>
   195→    request<ApiResponse<NotificationChannel[]>>(`/channels?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   196→  get: (id: string) => request<ApiResponse<NotificationChannel>>(`/channels/${id}`),
   197→  create: (input: ChannelInput) =>
   198→    request<ApiResponse<NotificationChannel>>("/channels", {
   199→      method: "POST",
   200→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   201→    }),
   202→  update: (id: string, input: ChannelInput) =>
   203→    request<ApiResponse<NotificationChannel>>(`/channels/${id}`, {
   204→      method: "PUT",
   205→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   206→    }),
   207→  delete: (id: string) =>
   208→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/channels/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   209→  test: (id: string) =>
   210→    request<ApiResponse<{ status: string }>>(`/channels/${id}/test`, {
   211→      method: "POST",
   212→    }),
   213→};
   214→
   215→// --- Todos ---
   216→
   217→export const todos = {
   218→  list: (status?: string, page = 1, perPage = 25, aggregated = false) => {
   219→    const params = new URLSearchParams({ page: String(page), per_page: String(perPage) });
   220→    if (status) params.set("status", status);
   221→    if (aggregated) params.set("aggregated", "true");
   222→    return request<ApiResponse<Todo[]>>(`/todos?${params}`);
   223→  },
   224→  get: (id: string) =>
   225→    request<ApiResponse<Todo>>(`/todos/${id}`),
   226→  acknowledge: (id: string, cascade = false) =>
   227→    request<ApiResponse<{ status: string }>>(`/todos/${id}/acknowledge${cascade ? '?cascade=true' : ''}`, { method: "PATCH" }),
   228→  resolve: (id: string, cascade = false) =>
   229→    request<ApiResponse<{ status: string }>>(`/todos/${id}/resolve${cascade ? '?cascade=true' : ''}`, { method: "PATCH" }),
   230→  reopen: (id: string) =>
   231→    request<ApiResponse<{ status: string }>>(`/todos/${id}/reopen`, { method: "PATCH" }),
   232→};
   233→
   234→// --- System ---
   235→
   236→export const system = {
   237→  health: () => request<HealthStatus>("/health"),
   238→  stats: () => request<ApiResponse<Stats>>("/stats"),
   239→  trend: (granularity: "daily" | "weekly" | "monthly" = "daily", days = 7) =>
   240→    request<ApiResponse<TrendData>>(`/stats/trend?granularity=${granularity}&days=${days}`),
   241→};
   242→
   243→// --- Discovery ---
   244→
   245→export const discover = {
   246→  github: (params?: { q?: string; language?: string }) => {
   247→    const search = new URLSearchParams();
   248→    if (params?.q) search.set("q", params.q);
   249→    if (params?.language) search.set("language", params.language);
   250→    const qs = search.toString();
   251→    return request<ApiResponse<DiscoverItem[]>>(`/discover/github${qs ? `?${qs}` : ""}`);
   252→  },
   253→  dockerhub: (params?: { q?: string }) => {
   254→    const search = new URLSearchParams();
   255→    if (params?.q) search.set("q", params.q);
   256→    const qs = search.toString();
   257→    return request<ApiResponse<DiscoverItem[]>>(`/discover/dockerhub${qs ? `?${qs}` : ""}`);
   258→  },
   259→};
   260→
   261→// --- Suggestions ---
   262→
   263→export const suggestions = {
   264→  stars: () =>
   265→    request<ApiResponse<SuggestionItem[]>>("/suggestions/stars"),
   266→  repos: () =>
   267→    request<ApiResponse<RepoItem[]>>("/suggestions/repos"),
   268→};
   269→
   270→// --- Onboarding ---
   271→
   272→export const onboard = {
   273→  scan: async (repoUrl: string): Promise<ApiResponse<OnboardScan>> => {
   274→    const res = await fetch(`${BASE}/onboard/scan`, {
   275→      method: "POST",
   276→      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
   277→      body: JSON.stringify({ repo_url: repoUrl }),
   278→    });
   279→    // 409 returns the existing active scan — treat as success
   280→    if (res.status === 409) {
   281→      return res.json();
   282→    }
   283→    if (!res.ok) {
   284→      const body = await res.json().catch(() => null);
   285→      throw new Error(body?.error?.message ?? `Request failed: ${res.status}`);
   286→    }
   287→    return res.json();
   288→  },
   289→  getScan: (id: string) =>
   290→    request<ApiResponse<OnboardScan>>(`/onboard/scans/${id}`),
   291→  apply: (id: string, selections: OnboardSelection[]) =>
   292→    request<ApiResponse<OnboardApplyResult>>(`/onboard/scans/${id}/apply`, {
   293→      method: "POST",
   294→      body: JSON.stringify({ selections }),
   295→    }),
   296→};
   297→
   298→// --- Release Gates ---
   299→
   300→export const gates = {
   301→  get: async (projectId: string): Promise<ApiResponse<ReleaseGate | null>> => {
   302→    const res = await fetch(`${BASE}/projects/${projectId}/release-gate`, {
   303→      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
   304→    });
   305→    if (res.status === 404) {
   306→      return { data: null };
   307→    }
   308→    if (!res.ok) {
   309→      const body = await res.json().catch(() => null);
   310→      throw new Error(body?.error?.message ?? `Request failed: ${res.status}`);
   311→    }
   312→    return res.json();
   313→  },
   314→  upsert: (projectId: string, input: ReleaseGateInput) =>
   315→    request<ApiResponse<ReleaseGate>>(`/projects/${projectId}/release-gate`, {
   316→      method: "PUT",
   317→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   318→    }),
   319→  delete: (projectId: string) =>
   320→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/projects/${projectId}/release-gate`, {
   321→      method: "DELETE",
   322→    }),
   323→  listReadiness: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
   324→    request<ApiResponse<VersionReadiness[]>>(
   325→      `/projects/${projectId}/version-readiness?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`
   326→    ),
   327→  getReadiness: (projectId: string, version: string) =>
   328→    request<ApiResponse<VersionReadiness>>(
   329→      `/projects/${projectId}/version-readiness/${encodeURIComponent(version)}`
   330→    ),
   331→  listEvents: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
   332→    request<ApiResponse<GateEvent[]>>(
   333→      `/projects/${projectId}/gate-events?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`
   334→    ),
   335→  listEventsByVersion: (projectId: string, version: string, page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
   336→    request<ApiResponse<GateEvent[]>>(
   337→      `/projects/${projectId}/version-readiness/${encodeURIComponent(version)}/events?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`
   338→    ),
   339→};
   340→

Explore the existing filter/listing patterns in the Changelogue frontend. I need to understand:

1. **Releases page** (`web/app/releases/`): How does it currently filter? Look for the "show excluded" toggle, any project filtering, date filtering, pagination, URL query param sync.

1→package api
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"net/http"
     5→	"strings"
     6→
     7→	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     8→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/auth"
     9→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
    10→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    11→)
    12→
    13→// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
    14→type Dependencies struct {
    15→	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
    16→	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
    17→	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
    18→	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
    19→	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
    20→	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
    21→	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
    22→	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
    23→	AgentStore            AgentStore
    24→	TodosStore            TodosStore
    25→	OnboardStore          OnboardStore
    26→	GatesStore            GatesStore
    27→	PublicURL             string
    28→	KeyStore              KeyStore
    29→	SessionValidator      SessionValidator
    30→	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
    31→	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
    32→	NoAuth                bool
    33→	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
    34→	HTTPClient            *http.Client
    35→}
    36→
    37→// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
    38→func RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
    39→	// Public chain: no auth required.
    40→	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)
    41→
    42→	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
    43→	chain := publicChain
    44→	if !deps.NoAuth {
    45→		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore, deps.SessionValidator))
    46→	}
    47→
    48→	// Projects (CRUD)
    49→	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
    50→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
    51→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
    52→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
    53→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
    54→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))
    55→
    56→	// Sources (nested under projects)
    57→	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
    58→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
    59→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
    60→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
    61→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
    62→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
    63→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))
    64→
    65→	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
    66→	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
    67→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
    68→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
    69→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
    70→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
    71→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))
    72→
    73→	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
    74→	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
    75→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
    76→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
    77→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
    78→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))
    79→
    80→	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
    81→	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
    82→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
    83→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
    84→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
    85→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
    86→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))
    87→
    88→	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
    89→	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
    90→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
    91→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
    92→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchCreate)))
    93→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchDelete)))
    94→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
    95→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
    96→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))
    97→
    98→	// Channels (CRUD + test)
    99→	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders(), deps.PublicURL)
   100→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))

237→func (s *PgStore) ListAllReleases(ctx context.Context, page, perPage int, includeExcluded bool) ([]models.Release, int, error) {
   238→	var total int
   239→	if includeExcluded {
   240→		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
   241→			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM releases r
   242→			 LEFT JOIN sources s ON r.source_id = s.id`).Scan(&total)
   243→		if err != nil {
   244→			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("count releases: %w", err)
   245→		}
   246→	} else {
   247→		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
   248→			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM releases r
   249→			 LEFT JOIN sources s ON r.source_id = s.id
   250→			 WHERE (s.version_filter_include IS NULL OR r.version ~ s.version_filter_include)
   251→			   AND (s.version_filter_exclude IS NULL OR r.version !~ s.version_filter_exclude)
   252→			   AND (s.exclude_prereleases = false OR r.raw_data->>'prerelease' IS NULL OR r.raw_data->>'prerelease' != 'true')`).Scan(&total)
   253→		if err != nil {
   254→			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("count releases: %w", err)
   255→		}
   256→	}
   257→	offset := (page - 1) * perPage
   258→	var rows pgx.Rows
   259→	var err error
   260→	if includeExcluded {
   261→		rows, err = s.pool.Query(ctx,
   262→			`SELECT r.id, r.source_id, r.version, COALESCE(r.raw_data,'{}'), r.released_at, r.created_at,
   263→			        COALESCE(p.id::text,''), COALESCE(p.name,''), COALESCE(s.provider,''), COALESCE(s.repository,''),
   264→			        CASE WHEN
   265→			          (s.version_filter_include IS NOT NULL AND r.version !~ s.version_filter_include)
   266→			          OR (s.version_filter_exclude IS NOT NULL AND r.version ~ s.version_filter_exclude)
   267→			          OR (s.exclude_prereleases = true AND r.raw_data->>'prerelease' = 'true')
   268→			        THEN true ELSE false END,
   269→			        COALESCE(sr_info.id::text,''), COALESCE(sr_info.status,''), COALESCE(sr_info.urgency,'')
   270→			 FROM releases r
   271→			 LEFT JOIN sources s ON r.source_id = s.id
   272→			 LEFT JOIN projects p ON s.project_id = p.id
   273→			 LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
   274→			     (SELECT sr.id, sr.status, sr.report->>'urgency' AS urgency, 0 AS priority
   275→			      FROM semantic_releases sr
   276→			      WHERE sr.project_id = p.id AND sr.version = r.version
   277→			      ORDER BY sr.created_at DESC LIMIT 1)
   278→			     UNION ALL
   279→			     (SELECT NULL::uuid, 'processing', '', 1
   280→			      FROM agent_runs ar
   281→			      WHERE ar.project_id = p.id AND ar.version = r.version
   282→			        AND ar.status IN ('pending', 'running')
   283→			      LIMIT 1)
   284→			     ORDER BY priority LIMIT 1
   285→			 ) sr_info ON true
   286→			 ORDER BY COALESCE(r.released_at, r.created_at) DESC LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2`, perPage, offset)
   287→	} else {
   288→		rows, err = s.pool.Query(ctx,
   289→			`SELECT r.id, r.source_id, r.version, COALESCE(r.raw_data,'{}'), r.released_at, r.created_at,
   290→			        COALESCE(p.id::text,''), COALESCE(p.name,''), COALESCE(s.provider,''), COALESCE(s.repository,''),
   291→			        false,
   292→			        COALESCE(sr_info.id::text,''), COALESCE(sr_info.status,''), COALESCE(sr_info.urgency,'')
   293→			 FROM releases r
   294→			 LEFT JOIN sources s ON r.source_id = s.id
   295→			 LEFT JOIN projects p ON s.project_id = p.id
   296→			 LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
   297→			     (SELECT sr.id, sr.status, sr.report->>'urgency' AS urgency, 0 AS priority
   298→			      FROM semantic_releases sr
   299→			      WHERE sr.project_id = p.id AND sr.version = r.version
   300→			      ORDER BY sr.created_at DESC LIMIT 1)
   301→			     UNION ALL
   302→			     (SELECT NULL::uuid, 'processing', '', 1
   303→			      FROM agent_runs ar
   304→			      WHERE ar.project_id = p.id AND ar.version = r.version
   305→			        AND ar.status IN ('pending', 'running')
   306→			      LIMIT 1)
   307→			     ORDER BY priority LIMIT 1
   308→			 ) sr_info ON true
   309→			 WHERE (s.version_filter_include IS NULL OR r.version ~ s.version_filter_include)
   310→			   AND (s.version_filter_exclude IS NULL OR r.version !~ s.version_filter_exclude)
   311→			   AND (s.exclude_prereleases = false OR r.raw_data->>'prerelease' IS NULL OR r.raw_data->>'prerelease' != 'true')
   312→			 ORDER BY COALESCE(r.released_at, r.created_at) DESC LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2`, perPage, offset)
   313→	}
   314→	if err != nil {
   315→		return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("list all releases: %w", err)
   316→	}
   317→	defer rows.Close()
   318→	var releases []models.Release
   319→	for rows.Next() {
   320→		var rel models.Release
   321→		if err := rows.Scan(&rel.ID, &rel.SourceID, &rel.Version, &rel.RawData, &rel.ReleasedAt, &rel.CreatedAt,
   322→			&rel.ProjectID, &rel.ProjectName, &rel.Provider, &rel.Repository, &rel.Excluded,
   323→			&rel.SemanticReleaseID, &rel.SemanticReleaseStatus, &rel.SemanticReleaseUrgency); err != nil {
   324→			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("scan release: %w", err)
   325→		}
   326→		releases = append(releases, rel)
   327→	}
   328→	return releases, total, nil
   329→}
   330→

   332→	var total int
   333→	if includeExcluded {
   334→		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
   335→			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM releases r
   336→			 JOIN sources s ON r.source_id = s.id
